review_execution.command: claude -p --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --tools "" --output-format json < staged diff prompt > /tmp/cp00-124-claude-review-output-valid.json

findings.title: Canonical 150-unit plan authority (closeout-pack-plan.json) is not part of this diff
findings.evidence: The pack's 150-unit range RP02.P06.M06.S09-RP02.P07.M03.S10 is asserted internally (PERMISSION_KERNEL_CP124_PACK_BINDING, contract JSON, manifest) and cross-checked by requirePlanPackUnitMatch(cp124PlanPack, createPermissionKernelCp124CoveredUnitIds(), "CP00-124"), but docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json is not included in the staged diff, so the plan-side unit binding cannot be independently confirmed from the diff alone.
findings.recommendation: Informational only; non-blocking. The runtime validator already fails closed if the plan lacks a matching CP00-124 entry (required=true). No code change needed — just confirm the plan file contains the CP00-124 row with the same range at closeout.

parsed_review.rationale: The diff is purely additive synthetic, metadata-only scaffolding consistent with the CP119–CP123 pattern and adds no forbidden runtime behavior. Unit accounting is correct and internally enforced: 89 permission-matrix terminal units (RP02.P06.M06–M10) + 61 failure-taxonomy opening units (RP02.P07.M00–M03) = 150, first unit RP02.P06.M06.S09, last unit RP02.P07.M03.S10, 9 micro-phases, with deliverable/area/domain counts all matching validatePermissionKernelCp124Coverage and the .mjs contract assertions. CP123 inheritance is exercised (createPermissionKernelCp123PermissionAuditTerminalBoundary + cross_tenant_test, asserting boundary_case_count 10 and reason 'cross_tenant_deny'). Permission-matrix guardrails are preserved: cross-tenant audit_hint.object_id redacted to 'redacted_cross_tenant_object', search/leak trimming returns only d_cp124_document with rendered_fields limited to id/type/matter and hidden_fields_rendered false (no unauthorized counts; omitted_result_policy = do_not_count_or_render_unauthorized_results), review/approval routes resolve to review_required_routing/approval_required_routing without executes_claude_review or grants_human_approval, export/download and external share resolve without execution, AI retrieval returns blocked_ai_retrieval_boundary, audit hints are preview_only with emitted_to_audit_ledger false, and matrix/fixture rows carry persisted:false. Failure-taxonomy cases fail closed across every required code (missing tenant/actor/Matter/resource, unknown action, cross-tenant, permission denied, ambiguous rule, stale reference, lock conflict, retry exhaustion, rollback, compensation, blocked-claim receipt, fixture/test/smoke, audit failure hint, Hermes failure evidence) with fail_closed true and retry/rollback/lock/compensation all flagged executed:false. All outputs flow through freezeNoWriteResult and a coverage loop asserting no policy mutation, no audit/product/database writes, no idempotency persistence, no lock/retry/rollback/compensation execution, no export/share/AI/analytics execution, and no LDIP — and the functions are pure (no I/O, network, fs, or DB), so the no-write attestation matches actual behavior rather than masking it. No runtime UI/API route is registered (only an index.js re-export, README docs, and contract metadata), and handoff is fixed to CP00-125 / RP02.P07.M03.S11 with hrx_embedded_boundary intact (no HRX split). The single P3 is an informational note that the external plan file is not in-diff; it is enforced at runtime and does not block closeout.
